zh.Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, Engl: 英國藍卡爾班可 霍爾 亞爾 普雷斯頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Moore於大約1508年出生在英國藍卡爾班可 霍爾 亞爾 普雷斯頓時，他的父親Walter Brenchley Moore35歲，母親Lady Alice Ireland39歲。他於1542年11月19日與Elizabeth Baynes結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1546年在他的家鄉享年40歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾坎普薩爾。

zh-hans.Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, Engl: 英国藍卡爾班可 霍爾 亞爾 普雷斯頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Moore于大约1508年出生在英国藍卡爾班可 霍爾 亞爾 普雷斯頓时，他的父亲Walter Brenchley Moore35岁，母亲Lady Alice Ireland39岁。他于1542年11月19日与Elizabeth Baynes结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1546年在他的家乡享年40岁，埋葬在英国英格兰越可爾坎普薩爾。

nameMeanings.name: Moore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Moor
meanings: English, Irish, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Moore  .
German: variant of   Mohr  .

Dutch (also De Moor): nickname for a man of swarthy complexion, from Middle Dutch moor ‘Moor’ (from Latin maurus), or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a Moor. Compare De Moor .

variants.name: More
meanings: French: nickname for a dark-skinned man, from a diminutive of Old French more ‘Moor’ (see More ).

variants.name: Morrin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Morin, a diminutive of More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus); see Moore 2 and 3.
English: perhaps sometimes from the Middle English personal name Morwin (Old English Mōrwine, the first element of which is uncertain and the second wine ‘friend’).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

variants.name: Moresco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Primo, Gino, Luciano, Riccardo.
Italian:
(Morésco): from a medieval personal name or a nickname for someone from North Africa or a swarthy or dark-haired man, from medieval Latin Mauriscus ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2).

variants.name: Morey
meanings: Irish (Limerick and Cork): variant of   Moore   4.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Mory, a pet form of Amauri (see Amery ).
English: perhaps also a variant of   Moore   1–3.

variants.name: Morrell
meanings: English (northern): from the medieval personal name Morel, a diminutive of Middle English or Old French More. Compare Moore .
Americanized form of French   Morel  . Compare   Morell   3.

variants.name: Moores
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of Moore , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McMoore
meanings: Irish: variant of Moore 4, in which Mac- ‘son of’ has been prefixed in modern times to give the name (Ó Mórdha ‘descendant of Mórdha’) an unmistakably Gaelic identity.

variants.name: Moro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Mario, Alberto, Jorge, Lino, Luis, Luiz, Manuel, Ramiro, Adolfo, Aida.  Italian Angelo, Pino, Pio, Remo, Antonio, Ciro, Cosmo, Elio, Enrico, Geno, Giancarlo, Gino.
Spanish: nickname or ethnic name from moro ‘Moor’, from Latin Maurus ‘Moor, North African’.
Italian: nickname from moro ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2, compare 1 above), also ‘black-haired man’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Moore was born about 1508, in Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, England, his father, Walter Brenchley Moore, was 35 and his mother, Lady Alice Ireland, was 39. He married Elizabeth Baynes on 19 November 1542. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 3 daughters. He died about 1546, in his hometown, at the age of 40, and was buried in Campsall, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, Engl: Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, Angleterre
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Moore est né environ 1508, à Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Walter Brenchley Moore, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Lady Alice Ireland, avait 39 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Baynes le 19 novembre 1542. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé environ 1546, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 40 ans, et a été enterré à Campsall, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, Engl: Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, England
de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Moore um 1508 in Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Walter Brenchley Moore 35 und seine Mutter Lady Alice Ireland 39. Er hat am 19. November 1542 Elizabeth Baynes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb um 1546 im Alter von 40 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Campsall, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ko.Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, Engl: 영국 랭카실르 뱅크 홀 니아어 플레스턴
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Moore(이)가 대략 1508년 영국 랭카실르 뱅크 홀 니아어 플레스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Walter Brenchley Moore(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Alice Ireland(은)는 39세였다. 그는 1542년 11월 19일 Elizabeth Baynes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1546년 그의 고향에서 40세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 캠프사에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Baynes em 19 de novembro de 1542. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 3 filhas. ru.Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, Engl: банк холл нир престон, ланкашир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Moore родился около 1508 г. в местности банк холл нир престон, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Walter Brenchley Moore было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Alice Ireland, 39. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Baynes 19 ноября 1542 г. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер около 1546 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 40 лет и был похоронен в местности кампсалл, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
